text: Audit-loop fire (2026-06-16 iter-6): RAN the audit-loop WORKFLOW in practice (honor repeated use-workflows; wf_b1e5891f-e5d, 9 agents, 378k tokens, 8.3min, 6 scans 3 clean 3 findings). VALUE-ADD: the workflow's residual scan found a repo-state defect the inline audit missed -- 8 orphan agent-worktree gitlinks (mode 160000) committed in gm main under .claude/worktrees/agent-* (added 73b159a9), all git-prunable + unlocked = dead orphans (prior fix b3f9018b left gitlinks on the false gitlink==live assumption). FIXED this fire: git_rm --cached the 8 gitlinks + broadened managed-gitignore .claude/worktrees/*/.gm/ -> .claude/worktrees/. LESSON: prunable+unlocked gitlinks are dead orphans -> untrack+ignore; gitlink != live worktree. WORKFLOW FLAW: its ccsniff triage MISLABELED the 7 search-discipline hits as 'foreign session 5c195878' but 5c195878 IS the parent (main-loop) session -- they are the parent's 4 iter-5 Greps + the workflow's OWN 2 subagent find/glob; generic workflow agent() calls are NOT codesearch-disciplined (they native-search) AND the triage cannot identify its own parent/subagent activity. So running the audit workflow both CAUSES search-discipline deviations and misattributes them -- a structural reason the inline gm-skill MO stays cleaner; the workflow's real strength is independent residual/cascade discovery. OWN search-discipline this fire: 4 iter-5 Greps on gm files (correction: codesearch verb + Read only). SKILL.md/AGENTS.md doc commits ship on the NEXT cascade (plugkit bump), not on direct gm commits -- a publish latency.
